Re:
Farn P olrrrcAI- PRACTTcES C ourrarssroN
P.O. Box 807 c 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fpx (916) 322-0886
January 22,2002
Peter A. Bagatelos
Bagatelos & Fadem, LLP
380 West Portal Avenue, Suite F
San Francisco, CA 94127
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01-297
Dear Mr. Bagatelos
QUESTION
Can a committee, primarily formed to support a particular candidate, cooperate
with the candidate and qualify as a "related committee" to the candidate's own controlled
committee without itself becoming a controlled committee?
CONCLUSION
If a committee is either directly or indirectly controlled by a candidate, it is a
controlled committee. The term "related committee" as used in Section 5 of Form 460 is
not intended to be substantively definitional of another category of committee under the
Act.
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 I 0 9 - 1 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.
This letter is in response to your request for written advice regarding the
campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Because your request does
not refer to a specific proceeding or decision, we are providing informal assistance.
Informal assistance does not confer immunity under section 83114. (Regulation 18329(c),
copy enclosed.)
.s=


File No. I-01-297
Page No. 2
FACTS
As an attorney who practices primarily in the area of politicallaw, you are
seeking clarification pertaining to various provisions of the Political Reform Act in order
to give proper and accurate advice to your clients on primarily formed committees and
controlled committees.
You have noted that on Form 460, there is a section entitled, "Related
Committees Not Included in this Statement." You have further noted that there is a "yes
or no" check box provided for response as to whether or not the "related committee" is a
"controlled committee." The possible existence of a "hybrid" category of "related
committee" that is not a controlled committee is the gravamen of your inquiry.
ANALYSIS
"Controlled committee" is defined at $ 82016, as follows:
"(a) 'Controlled committee' means a committee that is
controlled directly or indirectly by a candidate or state measure
proponent or that acts jointly with a candldate, controlled
committee, or state measure proponent in connection with the
making of expenditures. A candidate or state measure proponent
controls a committee if he or she, his or her agent, or any other
committee he or she controls has a significant influence on the
actions or decisions of the committee."
With regard to this definition, we have advised lhat a candidate exerts significant
influence on the actions of a committee if the candidate develops or implements
campaign strategy for the committee. (Madden Advice Letter, No. 4-85-197.)
The inclusion of the "Related Committees" section on the Form 460 is in no way
intended to supplant andlor supplement the definitional provisions of the Act, or to
otherwise create an additional substantive category of committee. It is simply as stated, a
solicitation of additional information regarding "any committees that are controlled by
[the candidate] or are primarily formed to receive contributions or make expenditures on
behalf of [his or her] candidacy."


File No. I-01-297
Page No. 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa
General
L. Williams,
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure

with the candidate and qualify as a "related committee" to the candidate's own controlled

CONCLUSION: If a committee is either directly or indirectly controlled by a candidate, it is a
controlled committee. The term "related committee" as used in Section 5 of Form 460 is
not intended to be substantively definitional of another category of committee under the
Act.
This letter is in response to your request for written advice regarding the
campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Because your request does
not refer to a specific proceeding or decision, we are providing informal assistance.
